title: Today in Spain: A roundup of the latest news on Monday
summary: Spaniards view Trump as bigger threat to world peace than Putin, ground staff strike causes luggage chaos at Barcelona airport and more news on Monday April 6th.

title: Can you do your driving test in Spain with an automatic car?
summary: Most non-EU drivers who move to Spain need to resit their driving test in order to be able to get behind the wheel here. But is there an option to do the practical exam with an automatic vehicle in a country where most cars are manual?

title: Spain's PM under fire as former top aide faces corruption trial
summary: A corruption trial of a former right-hand man to Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez begins on Tuesday in a politically explosive case that has threatened to topple the Socialist-led government.

title: More than Ikea: Where else to buy furniture in Spain
summary: Recently moved house and looking to furnish your new home in Spain? Here's where to look for furniture beyond the likes of Ikea.

title: Ex-matador killed by bull at Spanish arena ahead of bullfight
summary: A bull fatally gored a former matador who was helping to prepare a traditional Picasso-themed Easter weekend bullfight, organisers said Saturday.

title: Seven strange traditions celebrated at Easter in Spain
summary: Easter week is celebrated in Spain like nowhere else on earth, and Spaniards take their Holy Week traditions very seriously, even if some of them - to the outsider - seem a little bit bonkers.

title: Spain is tolerant, not racist, says Real Madrid coach Arbeloa
summary: Real Madrid coach Álvaro Arbeloa said Friday that Spain was a tolerant country and not racist, despite Islamophobic chanting during a national team match this week.
